title: polypodium pedunculatum
intro: Polypodium pedunculatum, also known as Autumn fern or Southern polypody, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its upright fronds and reddish-brown stipes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include the potential relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The herbal preparation of Polypodium pedunculatum is typically made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
